osm_tags.description: Sidi Qadi (or high judge) Ayyad came from Ceuta (or Sebta). He was the most famous scholar in Granada of the Malikite rite in the Muslim West. With the advent of the Almoravids, he was exiled in Tadla before moving to Marrakech where he lived until his de
osm_tags.name: Tomb of Qadi Ayad

osm_tags.description: Sidi Bel Abbas was born in Ceuta in 1129. In 1145, he moved to a hill of Igilliz just outside of Marrakech to live as a hermit, worshipping Allah in seclusion and only entering the city to attend Friday communal prayers.
osm_tags.name: Tombe de Sidi Bel Abbas

places.name: Tomb of Sidi Mohammed Ben Slimane Al-Jazouli

osm_tags.name: Tomb of Sidi Mohammed Ben Slimane Al-Jazouli

places.name: Tomb of Sidi Abdellah El Ghazouani

osm_tags.description: Sidi Ben Abdallah El Ghazouani came from the Berber Ghomara tribe. He consolidated the Sufi revival initiated by Sidi Ben Slimane el Jazouli.

osm_tags.price: Dh 10 plus Dh 10 to see the minbar.
